strengths: Strong Python + SQL ETL and pipeline experience
Proven automation and reporting impact with quantifiable results
Experience with data quality and CI/CD (Great Expectations, GitHub Actions)
Visualization skills (Power BI, Tableau) and stakeholder reporting
weaknesses: Shorter tenure in some roles (contract/project spans)
No mention of weekly lab meeting presentation experience
improvement_bullets: Architected production-grade ELT pipelines using Python and SQL within a Linux-based environment, leveraging Git and CI/CD workflows to manage complex data structures and numerical modeling datasets.
Applied statistical and numerical modeling techniques to 250K+ row datasets, utilizing Python (Pandas/NumPy) to conduct exploratory data analysis and segment high-value patterns.
Collaborated in cross-functional team settings to present data-driven reports during recurring weekly stakeholder meetings, translating complex technical findings into actionable research insights.
Optimized data storage and retrieval by implementing BigQuery partitioning and dbt modeling, ensuring efficient access for computational-heavy analytical workflows.
Developed robust data quality frameworks using Great Expectations to validate inputs for downstream numerical analysis, ensuring reliable and reproducible results.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Hourly Data Analyst - Ecohydrology Lab opportunity with New Jersey Institute of Technology. I am interested in this opportunity because of the lab’s reputation for rigorous empirical research and its focus on complex data-driven solutions to environmental challenges. What interests me about working for NJIT is its commitment to technological innovation and interdisciplinary research; this inspires me because my background in computer science and data engineering is uniquely suited to support the technical demands of ecohydrological modeling. I believe my expertise in large-scale data pipeline architecture and my proficiency in Python-based numerical analysis would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing clean, scalable, and automated data workflows. In fact, while architecting a production-grade ELT pipeline, I implemented dbt star schemas and automated quality validation using Great Expectations to handle time-series data. This work resulted in a 40% improvement in reporting efficiency and ensured that the data served as a reliable 'single source of truth' for stakeholder decision-making. Ultimately, I know that the Hourly Data Analyst - Ecohydrology Lab role at NJIT would allow me to bring my technical pipeline automation skills to the lab’s ongoing numerical modeling projects. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my communication and reporting skills. I regularly presented technical findings to non-technical stakeholders during weekly meetings, translating complex data into clear, actionable strategy. These sessions were instrumental in aligning the team’s goals and ensuring that data insights directly influenced organizational growth. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in big-data analytics in working on complex database exploration at NJIT. I know that my technical versatility and experience in managing data lifecycles align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
